questions.question: Explain the difference between $@ and $* in shell scripting with an example.
keyPoints: $* treats all arguments as a single string separated by IFS (usually space)
$@ treats each argument as a separate quoted string, preserving argument boundaries
When quoted, $* expands to one word, $@ expands to separate words matching original arguments
Example: If args are 'hello world' 'test', $* gives 'hello world test', $@ gives 'hello world' 'test'

questions.question: Write a shell script to check if a given number is even or odd.
keyPoints: Use modulo operator (%) to check remainder when divided by 2
If remainder is 0, number is even; else odd
Use arithmetic expansion $((number % 2)) or expr command

questions.question: Explain the various I/O redirection operators in shell with examples.
What are shell functions? How are they defined and called in a shell script?
keyPoints: Shell function is a reusable code block with a name
Syntax: function_name() { commands; } or function function_name { commands; }
Called simply by writing function name
Return using 'return' (exit status 0-255) or echo output

questions.question: Explain the case-esac statement in shell with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Syntax: case variable in pattern1) commands;; pattern2) commands;; esac
Patterns support wildcards and | for OR
;; indicates end of each case branch

questions.question: Write a shell script to find the largest of three numbers.
keyPoints: Accept three numbers as arguments or input
Use nested if-else or conditional operators
Compare first two, then compare result with third

questions.question: Explain the different types of loops available in shell scripting.
keyPoints: for loop: iterates over list of values or files
while loop: executes as long as condition is true
until loop: executes until condition becomes true
Syntax differences and use cases for each

questions.question: Write a complete shell script to display the contents of a file with line numbers. The script should accept filename as command-line argument and handle error conditions appropriately.
keyPoints: Use 'nl' or cat -n to display with line numbers
Use proper error messages to stderr

questions.question: Explain the process of debugging shell scripts. Discuss various methods and techniques with examples.
keyPoints: Use 'set -x' inside script to enable debugging
Use 'set -v' to print input lines as read

questions.question: Write a shell script that accepts a directory name as argument and displays all files with execute permission. Also display the total count of such files.
keyPoints: Use 'find' command or loop through ls

questions.question: Explain the use of grep, sed, and awk commands in shell programming with examples of each.
keyPoints: sed: stream editor - sed 's/old/new/' file for substitution
awk: text processing - awk '{print $1}' for column extraction
Combining these commands with pipes for complex operations
